rulings.Case Title: TELEKENEX, INC. VS. COLLABNET, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or, In The Alternative, To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 17, 2008, line 6. PLAINTIFF TELEKENEX, INC.'S Motion To Strike Or, In The Alternative, To Tax Costs. Hearing required to address a number of issues: How is it possible for the court to address the reasonableness of the work done when it is not grouped by task? For example, how much time was spent on the summary judgment motion, in preparing interrogatories, etc.? What evidence is there that the charges for library and IT staff are reasonable? Or are customarily charged in this market for this type of litigation? The voice and data charges do not appear to be actual charges but rather are an assignment of overhead to clients. How is it determined that this client should pay a particular amount? How much time was spent preparing the objection that is Ex. 17 to plaintiff's opposition? =(302/PJM/VC)

Hearing required to address a number of issues: How is it possible for the court to address the reasonableness of the work done when it is not grouped by task? For example, how much time was spent on the summary judgment motion, in preparing interrogatories, etc.? What evidence is there that the charges for library and IT staff are reasonable? Or are customarily charged in this market for this type of litigation? The voice and data charges do not appear to be actual charges but rather are an assignment of overhead to clients. How is it determined that this client should pay a particular amount? How much time was spent preparing the objection that is Ex. 17 to plaintiff's opposition? =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY, A CORPORATION VS. CHRISTOPHER E BUTLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Demurrrer To First Amended Complaint Of Christopher E Butler
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 17, 2008, line 8, DEFENDANTS ALLAN KAPLAN, I/PRO CORPORATION, Demurrrer To First Amended Complaint Of Christopher E Butler IS OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. THE COURT MAY NOT CONSIDER THE CONTENT OF INTERROGATORY RESPONSES IN DECIDING THE DEMURRER. THE ALTER EGO ALLEGATIONS ARE SUFFICIENTLY SET FORTH IN PARAGRAPHS 19, 20 AND 22. THE FRAUD ALLEGATIONS IN PARAGRAPH 16 ARE SUFFICIENT. PLAINTIFF'S WAGE CLAIM IS SUFFICIENTLY PLEAD IN PARAGRAPH 31. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Re Ntc And Mtn For Order Vacating And Setting Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 17, 2008, line 9, DEFENDANT MARTIN ENG'S Motion For Order Vacating And Setting Aside Default IS GRANTED BECAUSE OF THE PUBLIC POLICY IN FAVOR OF HEARING CASES ON THE MERITS. DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO REMIT $500 TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL FOR THE COST OF OBTAINING THE DEFAULT. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MCCORMACK BARON RAGAN MANAGEMENT SERVICES, VS. SHEILA KELLER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 17, 2008, line 14, PLAINTIFF MCCORMACK BARON RAGAN MANAGEMENT SERVICES, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE BASED ON THE POLICE REPORT AND CRIME LAB REPORT UNDER MCNARY V. DMV (1996) 45 CAL.APP.4TH 688. DEFENDANT FAILS TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. =(302/PJM/JW)
